title: Moving default-policy.json and default.yaml to containers/image
body:
All major distros (Fedora, debian, archlinux, gentoo, alpine, opensuse) are placing these 2 files in containers-common package. Why not fix it upstream?

1st step towards addressing https://github.com/containers/skopeo/issues/2170

comments.body: For the record, the files, and the Make rules to install them, were moved to https://github.com/containers/image/pull/2215 .
@mtrmac I guess we can revise this PR to handle any outstanding cleanup issues if @rahilarious is willing to continue this.

> @mtrmac I guess we can revise this PR to handle any outstanding cleanup issues if @rahilarious is willing to continue this.

I'm unfamiliar with cirrus stuff, any help would be appreciated
Ephemeral COPR build failed. @containers/packit-build please check.

> > @mtrmac I guess we can revise this PR to handle any outstanding cleanup issues if @rahilarious is willing to continue this.
> 
> I'm unfamiliar with cirrus stuff, any help would be appreciated

Ack, no worries. I'm working on enabling TMT tests in #1960  and if things work there we can probably get rid of cirrus tests that need changes here.
A friendly reminder that this PR had no activity for 30 days.

as per https://github.com/podman-container-tools/container-libs/blob/main/common/rpm/containers-common.spec , these files are being installed from `container-libs/image` repo so this PR should be merged now. 

PTAL @lsm5
@rahilarious there's a bash completion installation issue that's causing most (all?) tests to fail. PTAL.

> @rahilarious there's a bash completion installation issue that's causing most (all?) tests to fail. PTAL.

Being a Gentoo guy, I'd prefer not to fiddle with rpm spec files. But still, fixed. It's all green now

> Being a Gentoo guy, I'd prefer not to fiddle with rpm spec files. But still, fixed. It's all green now

Sorry, I should've been clear. I mainly meant the github actions jobs which will also need the completions target. Consider Packit / testing-farm as secondary CI, and thanks for the spec file change :slightly_smiling_face: .

The testing-farm ELN job failures can be ignored here. I'll probably just remove them if they keep failing.

> > Being a Gentoo guy, I'd prefer not to fiddle with rpm spec files. But still, fixed. It's all green now
> 
> Sorry, I should've been clear. I mainly meant the github actions jobs which will also need the completions target. Consider Packit / testing-farm as secondary CI, and thanks for the spec file change 🙂 .
> 
> The testing-farm ELN job failures can be ignored here. I'll probably just remove them if they keep failing.

Let me know if anything else is needed from my side

> Let me know if anything else is needed from my side

Yes, see previous comment about the failing github actions jobs that also need the `completions` target added in `doccheck` and `test_skopeo` jobs. These do not reuse the spec file. You'll see them in `.github/workflows/ci.yml`

@podman-container-tools/skopeo-maintainers @podman-container-tools/skopeo-reviewers PTAL as well.

If there are concerns about breaking current Makefile behaviour, maybe we could probably consider the `nobuild` approach: https://github.com/podman-container-tools/buildah/pull/6984

> https://github.com/podman-container-tools/skopeo/blob/main/install.md documents how users can use `make install`, and that would now be _insufficient_ to obtain a working installation

According to install.md user would 1st run `make` (also build completions now) then `sudo make install` which would only install completions. Nobody should be running `sudo make install` directly anyways.
_Almost nothing_ works if `policy.json` does not exist! So if `make install` from this project does not install it, the existing instructions are not sufficient.

> Yes, see previous comment about the failing github actions jobs that also need the `completions` target added in `doccheck` and `test_skopeo` jobs. These do not reuse the spec file. You'll see them in `.github/workflows/ci.yml`

done

> _Almost nothing_ works if `policy.json` does not exist! So if `make install` from this project does not install it, the existing instructions are not sufficient.

Skopeo depends on container-libs/image. policy.json should be installed by c/image. Should I add the line in install.md that c/image should be installed first?
